reasoning: The Wikipedia page currently shows that a Norovirus vaccine has not been created. The closest vaccine candidate to approval was Moderna's mRNA-1403, which entered Phase 3 trials in late 2024. However, as of mid-February 2025, the FDA placed this trial on a clinical hold due to Guillain-Barré Syndrome (GBS) side-effect risks. Even prior to this setback, Moderna only expected to gather sufficient efficacy data by the end of 2025 and submit an application for regulatory approval in early 2026. Other competitors (like Vaxart) are even further behind, only entering Phase 1 trials in early 2025. Therefore, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to be fully developed, approved, and updated on Wikipedia for the 2025 resolution dates, and extremely unlikely to happen before March 30, 2026. I assign the lowest possible probability (0.02) for the near-term dates, and only a very marginally higher probability (0.05) for the final March 2026 resolution date to respect the instruction to scale uncertainty with the time horizon.
